description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rough, angular profile and a predominantly brown coloration. The surface displays a mix of earthy tones, with lighter beige and white streaks running along one side, suggesting mineral veining or weathering. The stone's texture is coarse and uneven, with visible fractures and jagged edges, indicating it has been naturally broken rather than cut or shaped. One side features a relatively flat face, while the opposite side is more rugged and protruding. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban park, roadside, or landscaped area.
description: A rough, angular brown stone with white mineral streaks and a coarse, fractured surface. Its irregular shape and solid build make it suitable as a natural urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's broad, slanted face with visible mineral streaks and a scale for size reference.
description_view_1: This side shows the stone's narrow, upright profile with pronounced jagged edges and a darker, more fractured surface.
description_view_2: Here, the stone's bulkier, rounded side is visible, emphasizing its rough texture and solid mass.
description_view_3: This angle reveals the elongated, striated surface with prominent white veining and a more linear form.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular mineral veining and layering, with some linear white streaks and mottled brown areas.
attribute_weathering: Rough and fractured with visible signs of natural weathering, including surface pitting and minor erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, providing a subtle contrast to manicured or paved urban features.
